revision.text:
{{Short description|Group of burial mounds}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other burial mound cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category|Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano) }} *[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1158.html Nagano city cultural heritage database] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.matsushiro-year.jp/modules/sightseeing/index.php?action=FacilityView&facility_id=34 Matsushiro tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/soshiki/bunka/5763.html Nagano city home page] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Nagano (city)]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]

[[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Nagano (city)]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]]

{{Short description|Group of burial mounds}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other burial mound cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]

{{Short description|Group of burial mounds}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other burial mound cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other burial mound cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Group of burial mounds}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other burial mound cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[Category:円墳]]

{{Short description|Group of burial mounds}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other burial mound cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Group of burial mounds}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other burial mound cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Group of burial mounds}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other burial mound cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>

{{Short description|Group of burial mounds}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other burial mound cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]

Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.

There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>

{{Short description|Group of Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Group of Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.

Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.

Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.

There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>

The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano) }} *[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1158.html Nagano city cultural heritage database] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.matsushiro-year.jp/modules/sightseeing/index.php?action=FacilityView&facility_id=34 Matsushiro tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/soshiki/bunka/5763.html Nagano city home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ōmuro Kofun group]]}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[:en:Chikuma River|Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[Goguryeo]] or [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, [[bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano) }} *[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1158.html Nagano city cultural heritage database] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.matsushiro-year.jp/modules/sightseeing/index.php?action=FacilityView&facility_id=34 Matsushiro tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/soshiki/bunka/5763.html Nagano city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] or [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[JR East]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.

{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 大室古墳群244号墳.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the [[Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro]] neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large [[necropolis]] consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[:en:Chikuma River|Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), but at least 330 smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] or [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s include [[Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery, [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano) }} *[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1158.html Nagano city cultural heritage database] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.matsushiro-year.jp/modules/sightseeing/index.php?action=FacilityView&facility_id=34 Matsushiro tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/soshiki/bunka/5763.html Nagano city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ōmuro Kofun group]]}}

==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[Nagano Station]], take the [[Nagano Electric Railway|Nagaden Bus]], No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
