{{wikidata link|Q11434367}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Nagano (city)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano)]][[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[ja:大室古墳群 (長野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano)]][[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[ja:大室古墳群 (長野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano)]][[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[ja:大室古墳群 (長野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11434367]] Wikidata name: Ōmuro Kofun group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =大室古墳群244号墳.JPG|image= File:大室古墳群244号墳.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|36.58972222|N|138.23083333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano, Nagano]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849}}| region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the {{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|WD=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro}} neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large {{ill|Necropolis|en|Necropolis|WD=Q200141|lt=Necropolis}} consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[:en:Chikuma River|Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), but at least 330 smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}} or [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}} *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|Nagano Electric Railway|en|Nagano Electric Railway|WD=Q6958437|lt=Nagaden Bus}}, No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano) }} *[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1158.html Nagano city cultural heritage database] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.matsushiro-year.jp/modules/sightseeing/index.php?action=FacilityView&facility_id=34 Matsushiro tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/soshiki/bunka/5763.html Nagano city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano)]][[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[ja:大室古墳群 (長野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano)]][[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[ja:大室古墳群 (長野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano)]][[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[ja:大室古墳群 (長野市)]]
{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =大室古墳群244号墳.JPG|image= File:大室古墳群244号墳.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|36.58972222|N|138.23083333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano, Nagano]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}| region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the {{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|WD=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro|1=Matsushiro, Nagano}} neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large {{ill|Necropolis|en|Necropolis|WD=Q200141|lt=Necropolis|1=Necropolis}} consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[:en:Chikuma River|Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), but at least 330 smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}} or [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshin-etsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}} *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|Nagano Electric Railway|en|Nagano Electric Railway|WD=Q6958437|lt=Nagaden Bus|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}, No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|Nagano Electric Railway|en|Nagano Electric Railway|WD=Q6958437|lt=Nagaden Bus|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}, No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano)]][[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[ja:大室古墳群 (長野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano)]][[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[ja:大室古墳群 (長野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Nagano)]][[en:Ōmuro Kofun group]][[ja:大室古墳群 (長野市)]]
{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =大室古墳群244号墳.JPG|image= File:大室古墳群244号墳.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|36.58972222|N|138.23083333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano, Nagano]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}| region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the {{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|WD=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro|1=Matsushiro, Nagano}} neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large {{ill|Necropolis|en|Necropolis|WD=Q200141|lt=Necropolis|1=Necropolis}} consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[:en:Chikuma River|Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), but at least 330 smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}} or [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshin-etsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}} *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|Nagano Electric Railway|en|Nagano Electric Railway|WD=Q6958437|lt=Nagaden Bus|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}, No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]

Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}} or [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshin-etsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}} *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|Nagano Electric Railway|en|Nagano Electric Railway|WD=Q6958437|lt=Nagaden Bus|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}, No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
==Gallery== <gallery> 大室古墳群244号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 244 大室古墳群168号墳合掌形石室.JPG|Tomb 168 大室古墳群238号墳.JPG|Tomb 238 大室古墳群240号墳.JPG|Tomb 240 大室古墳群240号墳石室内部.JPG|Burial chamber of Tomb 240 大室古墳群176号墳.JPG|Tomb 276 大室古墳館.JPG|Ōmuro Kofun Museum </gallery>

{{Short description| part_of of Kofuns}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Ōmuro Kofun Group | native_name =大室古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =大室古墳群244号墳.JPG|image= File:大室古墳群244号墳.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Kofun No.244 is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōmuro Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|35|23|N|138|13|51|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|36.58972222|N|138.23083333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano, Nagano]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}| region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (archaeological park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Ōmuro Kofun Group'''|大室古墳群|Ōmuro kofun-gun}} is a group of late ''[[Kofun period]]'' [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the {{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|WD=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro|1=Matsushiro, Nagano}} neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1997.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162751 |title=大室古墳群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ōmuro Kofun Group is a large {{ill|Necropolis|en|Necropolis|WD=Q200141|lt=Necropolis|1=Necropolis}} consisting of over 500 tumuli spread across two valleys on the south side of the [[:en:Chikuma River|Chikuma River]] at an elevation of 350 to 500 meters approximately six kilometers southeast of Nagano city. The tombs were built over a 250-year period from the 5th to the 8th centuries. They have been grouped by archaeologists into five groups ( Kanaiyama, Kitatani, Kajo, Omurodani, and Kitayama) by their geographic location, stretching across an area of roughly 2.5 square kilometers. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
There is only one [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|keyhole-shaped ''kofun'']] ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}), but at least 330 smaller {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) made of stacked river stones from the Chikuma River. Most of these {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} have a diameter of approximately ten meters, and there is no other Kofun cluster in Japan where there are so many such {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-shaped ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in such a small area. These mounds can be divided into 40 different variations, some with a scallop-shaped stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], or with a triangular gabled burial chamber ceiling. Theories that these tombs were built by immigrants to Japan from the ancient Korean states of {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}} or [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] remain controversial. Excavated [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, armor, swords, horse fittings and jewelry. The number of horse bones found was unusually large, and included the skull of a horse which was buried in the vestibule of one of the horizontal burial chambers. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with a museum, the {{nihongo| '''Ōmuro Kofun Museum'''|大室古墳館|Ōmuro kofun-kan}} displaying some of the artifacts discovered. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Access== *By car, 15 minutes from the Nagano IC on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshin-etsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}} *By bus, 20 minutes on foot from {{nihongo|Ōmuro Station|大室駅|Ōmuro eki}} (there are sign posts from the bus stop). From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}}, take the {{ill|Nagano Electric Railway|en|Nagano Electric Railway|WD=Q6958437|lt=Nagaden Bus|1=Nagano Electric Railway}}, No. 8, the Suzaka-Yashima Line, departing from the south exit of Nagano Station.
